mnemonics.title: CARS Build Classes
mnemonics.content: C - Class keyword, A - Attributes defined, R - __init__() constructor, S - Self parameter. Remember: 'Construct A Robust Structure' for class creation steps.

mnemonics.title: Family Tree Inheritance
mnemonics.content: Parent classes pass DNA (methods/properties) to children. Visualize: Grandpa → Parent → Child with inherited traits like IoT Device → TemperatureSensor → OutdoorSensor.

mnemonics.title: Black Box Encapsulation
mnemonics.content: Imagine devices as sealed boxes: Properties (internal circuits) are private (_voltage), methods (buttons) are public. 'Don't touch the wires inside!'

mnemonics.title: IoT House Blueprint
mnemonics.content: Rooms = Classes (KitchenClass, BedroomClass), Devices = Objects (OvenObj, ThermostatObj). Methods = Actions (oven.bake(350), thermostat.set_temp(72))

acronyms.usage: Four OOP pillars for IoT device modeling
Common methods in IoT device classes (create_sensor(), read_data())

visualTips.visualization: Picture a Russian doll: Outer doll = class name, middle layer = attributes, innermost = methods
Draw a tree: Root = BaseDevice, branches = Sensor/Actuator classes, leaves = specific devices (MotionSensor, LEDActuator)
Draw a pill capsule: Outer shell = public methods (get_temperature()), inner medicine = private attributes (_calibration_factor)
Mind map with central Cloud class connected to Gateway class, linking to multiple Device classes with data streams

analogies.analogy: Blueprint (class) vs Constructed House (object). Same blueprint can build multiple houses with different wall colors (attribute values)
Smartphone models: BaseModel (call/text) → ProModel (adds camera) → UltraModel (adds 5G). Each inherits and extends features
Bank ATM: Public interface (buttons for withdrawal), private internals (vault mechanisms). Users interact only with allowed methods
Universal remote: Same 'power_button' method works differently for TV (toggles standby) vs AC (activates thermostat)
